glm-copilot.command.setVisionModel: GLM: Open Vision Proxy in Model Manager
glm-copilot.walkthrough.title: GLM for Copilot Chat
glm-copilot.walkthrough.description: Set up GLM models in Copilot Chat.
glm-copilot.walkthrough.setApiKey.title: Manage GLM models and connections
glm-copilot.walkthrough.setApiKey.descri:
Choose the default connection and manage credentials from one page.
[Open Model Manager](command:glm-copilot.manageModels)
glm-copilot.walkthrough.showModels.title: Show GLM models
glm-copilot.walkthrough.showModels.descr:
Review GLM model routes in the model manager. If a model is hidden, enable it in VS Code's Language Models manager.
[Open Model Manager](command:glm-copilot.manageModels)
[Open Language Models](command:workbench.action.chat.manage)
glm-copilot.walkthrough.advancedSettings:
Manage models, connections, and the Vision Proxy in the model manager. Diagnostics and experimental options remain in Settings.
[Open Model Manager](command:glm-copilot.manageModels)
[Open settings](command:glm-copilot.openSettings)
glm-copilot.config.title: GLM Copilot
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.descr: Versioned model and connection state managed by [GLM: Manage Models and Connections](command:glm-copilot.manageModels). Use the manager for normal changes; edit this advanced object directly only for automation or recovery. Values can be defined at User, Workspace, or Workspace Folder scope.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.versi: Configuration format version. Version 1 is the only supported value.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.defau: Default official connection and optional compatible Base URL.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.endpo: Default official GLM endpoint. It determines the region, billing channel, and wire protocol.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.baseU: Optional compatible Base URL for models using the `default` route. An empty string explicitly clears an inherited custom URL. Explicit official routes and `same-region-standard` always use official GLM endpoints.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.model: Per-model API ID, official endpoint route, and image input mode overrides. Record keys are Copilot model IDs.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.apiMo: Model ID sent to the selected API endpoint.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.endpo: Official connection route for this model. GLM-5V-Turbo supports Standard API routes only.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.visio: Image input mode: `proxy` sends a text description from the Vision Proxy; `native` resizes and sends image data directly under the 2.5 MiB binary context budget; `mcp` stores images to disk and leaves a file-path prompt, letting an image-capable MCP tool (e.g. the official zai-mcp-server) read them by path — best for text-only models where base64 would waste context.
glm-copilot.config.modelManagement.custo: Custom model definitions keyed by their canonical Copilot model ID. Model routes and image modes remain under `models`.
Removes an inherited custom model at this configuration scope.
Optional legacy-compatible ID. The containing record key is authoritative.
glm-copilot.config.baseUrl.description: Optional GLM API base URL override for models whose `modelEndpointOverrides` route is `default`. Explicit official model routes and `same-region-standard` do not use this value. Leave empty to use the selected global `endpoint` preset.
glm-copilot.config.endpoint.description: Default GLM connection channel. Each option uniquely resolves to a base URL and wire protocol (OpenAI `/chat/completions` or Anthropic `/v1/messages`). Models can override it through `modelEndpointOverrides`; a non-empty `baseUrl` only overrides models using the default route.
Domestic BigModel Coding Plan via OpenAI `/chat/completions` at `https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/coding/paas/v4`. Default.
glm-copilot.config.endpoint.chinaStandar: Domestic BigModel pay-as-you-go API via OpenAI `/chat/completions` at `https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4`.
glm-copilot.config.endpoint.chinaAnthrop: Domestic BigModel Coding Plan via Anthropic `/v1/messages` at `https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/anthropic`. May bypass stricter rate limits on certain models.
glm-copilot.config.endpoint.internationa: International Z.ai Coding Plan via OpenAI `/chat/completions` at `https://api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4`.
International Z.ai pay-as-you-go API via OpenAI `/chat/completions` at `https://api.z.ai/api/paas/v4`.
International Z.ai Coding Plan via Anthropic `/v1/messages` at `https://api.z.ai/api/anthropic`.
glm-copilot.config.maxTokens.description: Maximum number of output tokens per request. Set to 0 to use the API default. Useful for controlling costs.
glm-copilot.config.experimental.stabiliz:
**Experimental**: improve GLM context-cache hit rate by pre-activating available tools.
- When the enabled tools list changes across turns, this may improve GLM context-cache hit rate.
- Requests will include more function definitions, so input tokens may increase. Cache-hit input tokens are billed at a lower price, but still count toward usage.
- This may add internal preflight tool calls to the current Copilot chat history. If you switch to another model in the same conversation, that model provider may reject or mishandle the replayed history. Start a new chat if model switching behaves unexpectedly.

Use [Configure Tools](command:workbench.action.chat.configureTools) to **view and manage** your tool list:

- 64 or fewer enabled tools: usually no need to enable this unless the tool list still changes across turns.
- More than 128 enabled tools: not recommended. GLM supports at most 128 functions in one `tools` request. Consider disabling tools you rarely use.
glm-copilot.config.debugMode.description:
Controls what diagnostic information GLM Copilot writes. Token usage is always reported to Copilot when the API returns it.

- **Minimal** - Token usage only. No diagnostic logs or request dumps.
- **Metadata** - Privacy-safe diagnostic metadata (request hashes, prefix overlap, tool schema changes). Does not contain prompt text. View with [`GLM: Show Logs`](command:glm-copilot.showLogs).
- **Verbose** - Complete request payloads written to disk for local debugging. **Warning: contains sensitive prompt content.** View with [`GLM: Open Request Dumps Folder`](command:glm-copilot.openRequestDumpsFolder).
glm-copilot.config.debugMode.minimal.des: Token usage only. No diagnostic logs or dumps.
glm-copilot.config.debugMode.metadata.de: Privacy-safe metadata. Safe to share publicly. View with `GLM: Show Logs`.
glm-copilot.config.debugMode.verbose.des: Contains sensitive prompt content. For local debugging only.
glm-copilot.config.modelIdOverrides.desc: Override the API model ID sent for any built-in or custom GLM model. Defaults are prefilled with official GLM IDs; add custom model keys only when the endpoint expects a different API model name.
glm-copilot.config.modelIdOverrides.glm-: API model ID for GLM-4.6V-Flash, also used by automatic vision proxy mode.
glm-copilot.config.modelEndpointOverride: Select the official GLM connection channel for each Copilot model ID. `default` inherits the global Endpoint and optional Base URL. `same-region-standard` keeps the global China/International region but uses the pay-as-you-go Standard API. Model-specific routes never use the global Base URL override.
Connection route for this model. Choose the global default or an official GLM endpoint.
GLM-5V-Turbo is available only through the Standard API. Choose the Standard API in the default region or pin a China/International Standard endpoint.
glm-copilot.config.modelVisionModes.desc:
Controls how image attachments reach each Copilot model ID. This is independent of `modelIdOverrides` so changing an upstream API model name does not silently change image routing.

- **proxy**: A vision model describes the image and sends text to the selected model. This is broadly compatible but adds a request and may lose visual detail.
- **native**: Resizes images before Base64 encoding and sends them directly to the selected API model under a 2.5 MiB binary context budget. The endpoint must support images; failures do not fall back to the proxy.
glm-copilot.config.modelVisionModes.entr: Image input mode: `proxy` converts images to text through the Vision Proxy; `native` resizes and sends image data directly to the selected API model.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.descript: Additional GLM-compatible models shown in the Copilot model picker. Use either a string model ID or an object with `id`, optional display `name`, token limits, `toolCalling`, and `thinking`. `contextWindowTokens` represents a shared prompt + completion window and takes precedence over `maxInputTokens` when valid. Custom model IDs override built-in IDs in the picker and lookup. Configure each custom model's image handling independently with `modelVisionModes`.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.string.d: Custom model ID. Defaults are applied for name, token limits, tool calling, and thinking; configure image handling with `modelVisionModes`.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.id.descr: Model ID shown in VS Code and used for requests unless `modelIdOverrides` maps it to another API model ID.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.name.des: Display name. Defaults to the model ID.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.contextW: Shared prompt + completion context window. Must be greater than maxOutputTokens; when valid, maxInputTokens is derived from it.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.maxInput: Maximum input tokens shown to Copilot. Defaults to 200000.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.maxOutpu: Maximum output tokens shown to Copilot. Defaults to 131072.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.toolCall: Whether this custom model supports tool calling. Defaults to true.
glm-copilot.config.customModels.thinking: Whether this custom model supports thinking mode. Defaults to true.
glm-copilot.config.visionModel.descripti: Compatibility setting managed by the Vision Proxy view in [Model Manager](command:glm-copilot.setVisionModel). Stores the selected VS Code vision model as `vendor/id` when that source is active. In automatic mode, GLM-4.6V-Flash is tried first and this VS Code model is used as fallback. Legacy bare model IDs are read for compatibility.
glm-copilot.config.visionPrompt.descript:
Prompt sent to the vision proxy model when describing image attachments before forwarding them to GLM.

[Open Vision Proxy in Model Manager](command:glm-copilot.setVisionModel)
glm-copilot.config.imageHandlingPrompt.d: System-level instruction used in effective `mcp` vision mode. It tells the model how to handle attached images: read them on demand via an image-capable MCP tool, reuse prior analysis across turns (with a one-line digest), be honest when an image is missing or unclear, and ask for a fresh screenshot when needed. It is injected on every `mcp`-mode turn, including text-only turns, to keep the prompt cache prefix stable.
glm-copilot.config.imageStoredPrompt.des: Per-image prompt inserted into the conversation when an image is stored as a local file; tells the model where the file is and to reuse prior analysis only when its output type still matches. Supports placeholders: `{0}` = label (e.g. "Image" or "Image 2 of 3"), `{1}` = file path.
glm-copilot.config.mcp.zai-mcp-server.en: GLM official aggregated MCP server. Transport: stdio (`npx -y @z_ai/mcp-server@0.1.4`). Includes vision and other capabilities. Disabled by default — enable it with this application-level checkbox.
GLM official web search MCP server. Transport: http. Disabled by default.
glm-copilot.config.mcp.web-reader.enable: GLM official web content fetch MCP server. Transport: http. Disabled by default.
glm-copilot.config.mcp.zread.enabled.des: GLM official deep-read MCP server. Transport: http. Disabled by default.
Controls how locally stored MCP-vision images are cleaned up. **Manual**: images are kept until you run "GLM: Clean Up Stored Images". **7-day TTL**: images whose last-referenced time is older than 7 days are deleted automatically on activation. Reusing an image (re-sending or replaying a conversation that references it) refreshes its timer, so actively-used images survive.
glm-copilot.config.mcp.imageCapableTools: Exact runtime tool IDs explicitly declared image-capable for `mcp` vision mode. Official tools are recognized from their required local-image-path input schema, so generated MCP prefixes do not need to be guessed. Add a custom ID matching the exact `tool.name` exposed by VS Code or its request diagnostics. If no image-capable tool is available, the request uses the vision proxy when configured; otherwise it fails before removing the image.
glm-copilot.config.mcp.servers.descripti: GLM official MCP servers are built-in (use the per-server application-level checkboxes above to enable/disable). This object is for **advanced config only**: override fields (e.g. point a URL at a proxy) or add your own complete custom server definitions. Built-in server `enabled` here is ignored — use the checkboxes. API keys are injected from the stored key, never stored here.
